proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Proximal Femur Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite proximal femur model linking head, neck, trochanters, calcar, and hip joint.
Articular femoral head supplied largely by retinacular and circumflex vessels.
Intracapsular segment between femoral head and intertrochanteric region.
Extracapsular metaphyseal zone between neck and subtrochanteric diaphysis.
Medial proximal femur attachment for iliopsoas; key subtrochanteric landmark.
Lateral proximal femur with abductor insertions; intertrochanteric fixation landmark.
Dense medial femoral neck cortical buttress supporting head-neck load transfer.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Medial Femoral Circumflex Artery

metadata.description: Dominant retinacular blood supply to the femoral head; vulnerable in neck fractures.
Posterior hip nerve at risk during proximal femur exposure and reduction maneuvers.
Ball-and-socket articulation between femoral head and acetabulum.
Proximal femoral diaphyseal fracture distal to the lesser trochanter with high mechanical demands.
Intramedullary implant shared with intertrochanteric neighborhood for proximal femur fixation.
Operative intramedullary fixation procedure using a cephalomedullary nail.
Plate fixation option for selected subtrochanteric fracture patterns.
Subtrochanteric fracture classification by fracture line and comminution.
Transverse/beaking pattern suggesting bisphosphonate-related or low-energy atypical morphology.
Varus malalignment after subtrochanteric fracture with gait and abductor consequences.

relationship_metadata.note: subtrochanteric region defined distal to lesser trochanter

metadata.claim_text: Subtrochanteric fractures occur in a high-stress diaphyseal transition zone with significant varus and shortening forces.
Cephalomedullary nailing is the common operative answer for most subtrochanteric fractures on board-style questions.
Missing atypical subtrochanteric morphology in a low-energy patient on long-term bisphosphonates.
Posterior proximal femur exposure for plate fixation places the sciatic nerve at risk and requires deliberate protection.
Varus malunion after subtrochanteric fracture alters abductor tension and gait mechanics.
Describe fracture level relative to lesser trochanter, comminution, implant plan, reduction strategy, and weight-bearing restrictions.

metadata.trigger: Unstable subtrochanteric fracture pattern requiring intramedullary stabilization
metadata.action: Proceed with cephalomedullary nail fixation with attending-confirmed reduction and implant strategy

metadata.trigger: Atypical subtrochanteric morphology in low-energy patient with bisphosphonate or metabolic bone disease concern
metadata.action: Discuss extended workup, implant choice, and contralateral surveillance with attending before standard fragility pathway
